data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NFLX, EXPE and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday advanced, as tech got a boost on strong quarterly results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), the world's largest contract chipmaker. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday:

  • Shares of Expedia (EXPE) climbed 6.5% in pre-market trading, after a report that ride-hailing giant Uber (UBER) had considered a possible takeover of the travel agency. According to a Financial Times report late Wednesday, Uber (UBER) has talked to advisers in recent months about a bid for Expedia (EXPE) after an acquisition of the travel agency was broached by a third party. As per the report, there has been no formal approach from Uber (UBER) and the ride-hailing firm's interest was described as being at a very early stage.
  • All eyes will be on Netflix (NFLX) stock, with the streaming leader scheduled to report Q3 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the Los Gatos, Calif.-based company to earn $5.13 per share on revenue of $9.77B. The latter metric is anticipated to rise 14.3% Y/Y. Wedbush estimates 5.5M net subscriber additions globally for Netflix (NFLX) in the quarter, versus a consensus of 4.5M. Investors will also be looking at the performance of the streamer's cheaper advertising tier, and any comments on it.
  • Shares of Blackstone (BX) added about 1% ahead of the opening bell. The world's largest alternative investment firm delivered Q3 distributable earnings per share that surpassed expectations. Blackstone's (BX) funds attracted inflows of $40.5B in the quarter, and the firm invested or committed $54B of capital - the highest in over two years. "The third quarter also represented the highest amount of overall fund appreciation in three years," top boss Stephen Schwarzman said. See how BX performed in charts.
  • Elevance Health (ELV) stock plunged nearly 13% ahead of market open. The health insurer stoked further concerns about high medical costs in the overall sector after slashing its full-year adjusted profit guidance. Managed care players have been dealing with increased costs associated with Medicaid plans as more patients seek medical procedures following the end of the pandemic-era policies. Chief executive Gail Boudreaux noted "unprecedented challenges" in the Medicaid business.

More on the markets

  • U.S. Equities In 2024: Invincibility Syndrome
  • Bearish Technical Patterns Emerge In Semiconductors And The Nasdaq
  • Are You Worried About An Overvalued Market?
  • Dow notches record close, S&P rises as small-cap rally, rotation helps Wall Street rebound
  • The U.S. budget deficit: Where it's at today

data.title: Iron ore sinks to three-week low as China demand sentiment wanes
data.description:
Iron ore futures fell below $100/ton in Asia on Thursday, as investors doubt whether China's latest stimulus moves will do enough to boost construction activity and steel demand.

China's housing minister announced a plan to expand a program to support the completion of unfinished housing projects to 4T yuan (~$560B), as the government tries to ease the country's real estate crisis.

However, analysts said the policies are focused on resolving the backlog of housing inventory, which provides little help for steel demand in the short term.

Iron ore futures (SCO:COM) in Singapore slumped 5% to $99.50/ton to the lowest since September 26, while copper, aluminum and zinc all dropped at least 1% on the London Metal Exchange.

Iron ore had rallied from a two-year low below $90/ton in late September to above $110, but prices have faded as a series of government briefings on economic policy fell short of expectations.

China's economy remains under pressure, with Q3 growth likely coming in at its weakest pace in six quarters, according to a Bloomberg survey.

Potentially relevant stocks include BHP (NYSE:BHP), Rio Tinto (RIO), Vale (VALE), Fortescue (OTCQX:FSUMF), Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) and Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY).

data.title: Voyageur announces proposed private placement
data.description:
• Voyageur Pharmaceuticals (OTCPK:VYYRF) on Thursday announced a proposed non-brokered private placement of up to 16.67M units at a price of $0.06 per unit, for gross proceeds of up to CND$1M.
  • Each unit shall be comprised of one common share in the capital of the corporation and one common share purchase warrant, each warrant exercisable into one common share at a price of $0.10 per share for thirty-six months from the date of issuance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ServiceNow gains as Oppenheimer ups target on 'positive checks' ahead of Q3
data.description:
• ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) rose 1% in premarket trading on Thursday as Oppenheimer raised its price target on the cloud software giant, citing "positive" checks going into the company's third-quarter results.
  • "Based on our preview mosaic, ServiceNow executed well and had a strong 3Q," analyst Brian Schwartz wrote in a research note. "However, next week's earnings set-up looks less favorable, with multiples already predicting good 3Q results and a materially successful outcome for the business over the FTM. In addition, there is a high 2025 subscription revenue growth expectation. In our view, management's 4Q guidance is unlikely to raise growth estimates and may underwhelm investors."
  • Schwartz, who reiterated his Buy rating and upped his price target to $1,020 from $825, said the pending third-quarter release "will likely reaffirm investors’ positive opinion" on the company and its momentum.
  • "Further, the company is also seen as a winner in the generative AI era and is executing well in the current operating environment," Schwartz wrote. "We remain bullish on ServiceNow over the long term."
  • ServiceNow is slated to report third-quarter results after the close of trading on Oct. 23. A consensus of analysts expect the company will earn $3.45 per share on $2.75B in revenue.

data.title: Nasdaq futures rise after robust results from TSMC, strong retail sales
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Thursday, with Nasdaq futures leading the pack, after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and robust retail sales, helped revive investor sentiments.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.6%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.9% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.07%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.01%.

Initial jobless claims matched and fell to 241K in the past week, while U.S. retail sales climbed more than expected in September. The Philly Fed outlook came in at +10.3 in October.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley (MS).

"Markets put in a solid performance yesterday, with investors remaining upbeat as they digested several earnings results and another modest decline in oil prices," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) shares were up 7% in premarket trade, after it beat both its Q3 bottom and top line as it continued to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom, and helped traders renew their confidence in tech stocks.

The September industrial production and the Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

"Manufacturing output (on the official data) has risen slightly this year. Sentiment (ISM, Philly Fed) has pointed to a near continuous contraction for the past two years. It is enough to make one question whether sentiment lives in the real world," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Philly Fed outlook +10.3 in October
data.description:
• October Philly Fed Business Outlook: +10.3 vs. +4.2 consensus, +1.7 prior.
  • The diffusion index for future general activity +36.7 vs. prior +15.8.
  • The employment index -2.2 vs. +10.7.
  • The future capital expenditures index was 23.50 vs. prior +25.
  • New Orders +14.2 vs. prior -1.5.

More on economic indicators

  • U.S. retail sales climb more than expected in September
  • Initial jobless claims, matching consensus, fall to 241K in past week

data.title: ASML removed from top picks at Cantor Fitzgerald after weak guidance
data.description:
• ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) was in the spotlight again on Thursday after Cantor Fitzgerald removed the chip equipment maker from its top picks, following its weak guidance earlier this week.
  • Shares rose 0.5% in premarket trading but have dropped 17% over the past five trading sessions.
  • ASML's shares are likely in the "penalty box for a bit" until investors can regain confidence for growth into 2026, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C. J. Muse said. As such, he believes shares will be range-bound between €600-700.
  • Muse kept his Overweight rating, however, as shares are "near trough-like levels" and there is a path over the next 6 to 12 months that could see the stock return to roughly €750.
